select_when: The evidence base is dominated by winners, survivors, or visible success stories, and the hidden denominator may change the conclusion materially.
A benchmark, case set, or strategy playbook feels compelling mainly because the failures are absent from view.
The next decision should widen the sample before drawing lessons from a vivid but incomplete success narrative.
The work would improve if the team explicitly checked what non-survivors, failures, or unseen cases would do to the base rate.
avoid_when: The anti-bias correction is becoming so broad that meaningful differences between real winners and real losers are being ignored rather than analyzed.
The denominator is already explicit and the team now mainly needs a decision, not another generic warning about incomplete samples.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on missing-denominator detection, outsider base rates, and anti-success-story distortion rather than on generic skepticism language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor statistics sprawl because the raw source is stronger on survivor-only evidence, outside-view correction, and anti-incomplete-sample discipline.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans organizational learning, talent assessment, and simulation-driven validation.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on hidden denominators, winner-only samples, and the need to bring in non-survivor data before concluding.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are grounded in source warnings about overcorrecting so broadly that real performance differences disappear.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on evidence with missing denominators, while the typed input remains a compact reviewed label.

output_type.note: The source yields an outside-view, denominator-aware correction; the output label compresses that into an operational artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Survivorship-bias is strongest as probabilistic and diagnostic correction, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
